title: DeepSeek LLM: Scaling Open-Source Language Models with Longtermism
task: frontier llm training; open scaling; dense pretraining
modules: dense transformer; scaling recipe; bilingual corpus; instruction tuning
strengths: open scaling evidence; strong dense baseline; bilingual coverage
weaknesses: older dense architecture; weaker long context; dated post-training
notes: Useful as an early open scaling anchor and baseline for later DeepSeek system papers.

title: OLMo: Accelerating the Science of Language Models
task: frontier llm training; open recipe; transparent pretraining
modules: open data pipeline; dense transformer; full training logs; evaluation harness
strengths: full transparency; reproducible training; strong scientific baseline
weaknesses: dense architecture; smaller scale than frontier; limited post-training
notes: Best fully open reference stack for controlled experimentation.

title: 2 OLMo 2 Furious
task: frontier llm training; open recipe; improved pretraining
modules: data curation; optimizer recipe; annealing schedule; dense transformer
strengths: strong fully open checkpoints; improved curation; reproducible recipes
weaknesses: dense architecture; not MoE; reasoning still relies on post-training
notes: Good fully open upgrade path over OLMo with stronger data and optimization choices.

title: OpenELM: An Efficient Language Model Family with Open-source Training and Inference Framework
task: frontier llm training; efficient dense architecture; open release
modules: layer-wise scaling; grouped query attention; efficient decoder; open recipe
strengths: efficient scaling; compact deployment; transparent release
weaknesses: smaller scale; limited reasoning depth; modest long-context story
notes: Useful for efficiency-first ablations and compact distillation targets.

title: The FineWeb Datasets: Decanting the Web for the Finest Text Data at Scale
task: frontier llm training; data curation; web-scale pretraining
modules: web filtering; deduplication; quality heuristics; open dataset
strengths: massive deduplicated corpus; strong accessibility; high-quality filtering
weaknesses: web bias; quality not task-aware; no direct reward signal
benchmarks: MMLU; ARC-C; HellaSwag; downstream LM eval
notes: Strong modern data foundation and a good source for stage-aware data-selection ideas.

title: DataComp-LM: In search of the next generation of training sets for language models
task: frontier llm training; data curation; dataset selection
modules: data selection benchmark; quality scoring; subset optimization; training set search
strengths: benchmarked data selection; strong curation signal; systematic comparison
weaknesses: expensive candidate scoring; benchmark alignment risk; mostly pretraining-focused
notes: Treat as the principled data-selection counterpart to FineWeb-style large-scale curation.

title: Magpie: Alignment Data Synthesis from Scratch by Prompting Aligned LLMs with Nothing
task: frontier llm training; synthetic alignment data; post-training
modules: aligned teacher prompting; self-instruct generation; data filtering; synthetic chat corpus
strengths: low-cost synthetic instruction data; open pipeline; diverse alignment data
weaknesses: teacher bias; synthetic drift; limited verifier control
notes: Natural bridge between data generation and post-training research.

title: DeepSeek-V2: A Strong, Economical, and Efficient Mixture-of-Experts Language Model
task: frontier llm training; efficient MoE; long context capable
modules: MoE routing; multi-head latent attention; economical training; load balancing
strengths: economical MoE; strong cost-performance; long-context support
weaknesses: routing instability risk; reasoning needs stronger post-training; engineering complexity
notes: Critical precursor to DeepSeek-V3 and the MLA plus MoE line.

title: OLMoE: Open Mixture-of-Experts Language Models
task: frontier llm training; open MoE; transparent sparse pretraining
modules: open MoE routing; sparse experts; open trainer; eval harness
strengths: open MoE baseline; reproducible sparse training; modular code
weaknesses: smaller than proprietary frontier; limited long-context; weaker reasoning polish
notes: Best open sparse baseline for controlled MoE experiments.

title: DeepSeek-V3 Technical Report
task: frontier llm training; frontier MoE pretraining; efficient reasoning-capable base model
modules: MoE routing; auxiliary-loss-free balance; multi-token prediction; multi-head latent attention
strengths: frontier cost-quality tradeoff; stable large-scale training; strong base model
weaknesses: huge systems complexity; closed data details; reasoning still depends on RL
notes: The strongest open-style base-model systems paper in the pool.

title: Tulu 3: Pushing Frontiers in Open Language Model Post-Training
task: frontier llm training; open post-training; alignment and reasoning
modules: SFT; preference tuning; rejection sampling; synthetic preference data
strengths: open post-training recipe; strong ablation stack; reproducible alignment pipeline
weaknesses: reward quality ceiling; multi-stage cost; weaker deep reasoning than RLVR
notes: Useful as the open post-training baseline and integration bed for new ideas.

title: Nemotron-4 340B Technical Report
task: frontier llm training; synthetic data curation; reward modeling
modules: synthetic data generation; reward model; preference data; distillation
strengths: strong synthetic-data pipeline; reward model family; open release
weaknesses: heavy teacher stack; expensive post-training compute; less transparent data mix
notes: Useful for data-engineering-heavy post-training and reward-model baselines.

title: Self-Rewarding Language Models
task: frontier llm training; self-rewarding alignment; iterative post-training
modules: LLM-as-judge; iterative DPO; self-generated preference labels; self-improvement loop
strengths: removes fixed external reward bottleneck; iterative self-improvement; flexible judge reuse
weaknesses: judge drift; reward hacking risk; saturation across iterations
notes: Important seed for self-improving reward loops but novelty is now crowded.

title: Meta-Rewarding Language Models: Self-Improving Alignment with LLM-as-a-Meta-Judge
task: frontier llm training; self-improving reward models; iterative post-training
modules: meta-judge; reward self-improvement; preference optimization; iterative labeling
strengths: meta-judge improves reward quality; explicit self-improving alignment; stronger judge adaptation
weaknesses: extra judge-training cost; meta-overfitting risk; evaluation leakage risk
notes: Good follow-up that exposes the next bottleneck after basic self-rewarding.

title: Process-based Self-Rewarding Language Models
task: frontier llm training; process-level self-rewarding reasoning
modules: process rewards; stepwise verifier; self-rewarding loop; reasoning supervision
strengths: step-level credit assignment; stronger reasoning signal; process supervision
weaknesses: annotation or rollout cost; longer training loops; brittle process decomposition
notes: Useful for moving self-rewarding beyond outcome-only scoring.

title: DeepSeek-R1: Incentivizing Reasoning Capability in LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
task: frontier llm training; reasoning RL; verifiable post-training
modules: RLVR; rule-based rewards; cold-start SFT; reasoning trace exploration
strengths: RL on verifiable rewards unlocks strong reasoning; clear gains; strong open reference
weaknesses: verbose traces; reward exploitation risk; weaker on non-verifiable domains
notes: The dominant open reasoning-RL endpoint and an unavoidable baseline.

title: Absolute Zero: Reinforced Self-play Reasoning with Zero Data
task: frontier llm training; zero-data reasoning self-play
modules: self-play task generation; verifiable rewards; autonomous curriculum; RL reasoning
strengths: zero-data self-play; autonomous curriculum; low human data dependence
weaknesses: self-play distribution shift; task grounding risk; evaluation alignment fragile
notes: Interesting because it pushes reasoning without curated human data.

title: LongRoPE: Extending LLM Context Window Beyond 2 Million Tokens
task: frontier llm training; long-context extension; efficient context scaling
modules: RoPE rescaling; progressive extension; readjustment fine-tuning; context interpolation
strengths: extreme context extension; progressive scaling; short-context readjustment
weaknesses: short-context regression risk; interpolation heuristics; retrieval grounding unchanged
notes: Best practical context-window extension seed in the pool.

title: Titans: Learning to Memorize at Test Time
task: frontier llm training; long-context memory; test-time learning
modules: neural memory; surprise-based update; persistent memory; test-time adaptation
strengths: trainable test-time memory; persistent memory beyond window; novel memory interface
weaknesses: memory optimization complexity; limited open reproduction; unclear fit with standard decoder stacks
notes: High-upside memory idea but with real systems risk.

title: ATLAS: Learning to Optimally Memorize the Context at Test Time
task: frontier llm training; long-context memory; optimized test-time memorization
modules: memory selection policy; test-time memory optimization; context compression; adaptation
strengths: improved memorization policy; sharper optimization story; strong follow-up to Titans
weaknesses: very new; open tooling immature; heavy test-time machinery
notes: Use as both a frontier seed and a caution that Titans-style memory is already moving quickly.

title: Mamba: Linear-Time Sequence Modeling with Selective State Spaces
task: frontier llm training; efficient long-sequence architecture
modules: selective state space; recurrent state; linear-time scan; hardware-efficient sequence model
strengths: linear-time sequence modeling; strong efficiency; compact recurrent state
weaknesses: tooling less mature than transformers; hybridization often needed; limited mainstream LLM adoption
notes: Useful precursor and contrast class for transformer-based efficiency work.

title: Transformers are SSMs: Generalized Models and Efficient Algorithms Through Structured State Space Duality
task: frontier llm training; efficient sequence modeling; unified architecture theory
modules: structured state space duality; generalized sequence operator; efficient algorithm; attention-SSM bridge
strengths: unifies attention and SSM views; hardware-efficient algorithms; strong theory link
weaknesses: translation to frontier LLM training is nontrivial; abstraction is heavier than direct systems work
benchmarks: PG19; The Pile; Long Range Arena
notes: Useful framing paper when bridging attention and state-space ideas.

title: Native Sparse Attention: Hardware-Aligned and Natively Trainable Sparse Attention
task: frontier llm training; long-context efficient attention; trainable sparsity
modules: native sparse attention; hardware-aligned kernels; learned sparse pattern; long-context training
strengths: trainable sparse attention; hardware alignment; strong long-context efficiency
weaknesses: kernel engineering burden; sparse patterns may underfit local detail; stack not yet ubiquitous
notes: One of the most actionable 2025 long-context efficiency papers.

title: Kimi Linear: An Expressive, Efficient Attention Architecture
task: frontier llm training; long-context efficient attention; linear attention
modules: KDA; expressive linear attention; hybrid layer design; kernel optimization
strengths: expressive linear attention; strong throughput; hybrid attention design
weaknesses: custom kernels; limited open reproduction at scale; task-specific tuning likely
notes: Represents the strongest recent linear-attention frontier signal.
